info.name: Fetch fixes for vulnerabilities in a repository, scan, or uploaded manifest

params.name: repo_slug
params.description: The slug of the repository to fetch fixes for (e.g. "my-repo" or "my-org/my-repo"). Use the full org/repo path to disambiguate when multiple GitHub orgs share the same repo name. Computes fixes based on the latest scan on the default branch

params.name: full_scan_id
params.description: The ID of the scan to fetch fixes for

params.name: tar_hash
params.description: A tarball hash from the upload-manifest-files endpoint. Mutually exclusive with repo_slug and full_scan_id.

params.name: vulnerability_ids
params.description: Comma-separated list of GHSA or CVE IDs, or "*" for all vulnerabilities

params.name: allow_major_updates
params.description: Whether to allow major version updates in fixes

params.name: minimum_release_age
params.description: Minimum release age for fixes packages (e.g., "1h", "2d", "1w"). Higher values reduces risk of installing recently released untested package versions.

params.name: include_details
params.description: Whether to include advisory details in the response

params.name: include_responsible_direct_dependencies
params.description: Set to include the direct dependencies responsible for introducing the dependency or dependencies with the vulnerability in the response

params.name: include_all_detected_ghsas
params.description: Set to include an allDetectedGhsas field listing every GHSA detected in the project, regardless of the vulnerability_ids filter. Useful for CLI clients that request a specific GHSA and want to show the user which GHSAs actually exist when the request has no overlap.

params.name: autofix_run_id
params.description: The id of an autofix-or-upgrade-cli-run record (created via /fixes/register-autofix-or-upgrade-cli-run) to associate this computation with. When set, the server records per-GHSA fix-computation telemetry into autofix_compute_vulnerability and updates the run's autofix_run row, mirroring the legacy /v0/fixes/compute-fixes endpoint. The caller must own the run's organization; foreign-org or unknown ids return 404.

items.docs:
Fetches available fixes for vulnerabilities in a repository, scan, or uploaded manifest.
Requires exactly one of repo_slug, full_scan_id, or tar_hash, as well as vulnerability_ids to be provided.
vulnerability_ids can be a comma-separated list of GHSA or CVE IDs, or "*" for all vulnerabilities.

## Response Structure

The response contains a `fixDetails` object where each key is a vulnerability ID (GHSA or CVE) and the value is a discriminated union based on the `type` field.

### Common Fields
